stem: You are running a variational algorithm: each iteration submits one Estimator job whose circuit parameters are chosen by a classical optimizer from the previous iteration's expectation value. You want to avoid re-queuing between iterations and keep the QPU responsive to the tight classical-quantum feedback loop. Which execution mode is the best fit?

options.text: Job mode (submit each iteration as a separate individual job)
Combine all iterations into a single primitive job

explanation.correct: A session gives dedicated, exclusive access to the QPU for a window, so iterative workloads whose next input depends on the previous result run back-to-back without each job re-entering the fair-share queue. This is exactly the recommended use case for sessions: iterative workloads that require frequent classical-quantum communication.

distractors.B: Batch mode is for independent jobs that are all available at the outset; here each iteration's parameters depend on the previous result, so the jobs cannot be submitted together and batching provides no benefit.
distractors.C: Submitting each iteration as a separate job sends every iteration back through the queue, which maximizes wall-clock time and device drift — the opposite of the goal.
distractors.D: The iterations cannot be a single job: the parameters for iteration n+1 are computed classically from iteration n's result, so they are not known when the job would be submitted.
